title: Circuit de Monaco
sub: The jewel of the calendar: 3.3km of armco-lined street through Monte Carlo, barely changed since 1929. Impossible to pass, impossible to forgive, the ultimate test of qualifying nerve.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Sainte-Dévote and the climb to Casino, then down through Mirabeau to the Fairmont Hairpin, the slowest corner in F1.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: Down to Portier, the harbourside tunnel at top speed, then hard braking for the Nouvelle Chicane and Tabac.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: The swimming pool complex, La Rascasse and Anthony Noghès, all flat-out concentration to the line.

tyreAlloc: Softest in the range · C3 / C4 / C5
stratNote: No tyre cliff and almost no degradation: tyres last forever. Strategy is dictated by the two-stop rule and safety-car timing, not wear.
spots: After the tunnel + the only DRS zone. Effectively the lone real passing chance.
Slowest corner in F1; a desperate lunge sometimes works.
Most positions change in the pits or via a safety car, not on track.
otNote: The hardest track to overtake on, full stop. Modern cars barely fit two-wide. Qualifying is worth more here than anywhere; pole usually converts to victory.
corners: Tight right off the main straight; first-lap pinch at the foot of the hill.
Flat-out climb up the hill toward Casino Square.
Blind left crest into Casino Square.
Right past the famous casino; bumpy and off-camber.
Downhill right tightening toward the hairpin.
The slowest corner in F1, ~45 km/h in first gear.
Right onto the run into the tunnel; exit speed critical.
Long, fast right in the dark; the lap’s top speed.
Heavy braking after the tunnel + DRS. The only genuine overtaking spot.
Quick left along the harbour, walls close on both sides.
Flick left-right into the pool complex; hugely committed.
Right-left exit; clip the kerbs, miss the walls.
Tight, slow right around the restaurant; traction-critical.
Final right launching onto the start/finish straight and DRS.
geo: The layout has barely moved in nearly a century. Public roads, harbour front, a tunnel and zero run-off.
A 42m swing: the climb from Sainte-Dévote to Casino, then the plunge through Mirabeau and the tunnel to harbour level.
In 2026 Monaco is the one weekend with active aero disabled all lap, locking max downforce. Pure mechanical grip.
With overtaking near-impossible, Saturday decides Sunday. A perfect lap between the walls is the truest test.
